interpretation: On real road graphs the cluster-size exponent is tau_F = 2.13 at zero added crossings, close to the two-dimensional value 2.055. Adding only a few percent of random crossing edges drives it to the mean-field value 2.5 and above. The finite-size read-out therefore tracks the local non-crossing constraint: removing that constraint moves the tested road windows from the near-two-dimensional end toward the mean-field end of the dial. This is the controlled counterpart to the cross-domain contrast and supports the mechanism without claiming a theorem or a high-precision universality proof.
